Fernandezia falcifolia is further categorized as a genus within the orchid family, which is native to South America. The genus Fernandezia is a part of the subtribe Oncidiinae, a group of small to medium-sized orchid genera. Further classification within the subtribe is still being researched and refined by botanists and taxonomists.

intro: Fernandezia falcifolia, also known as the "Fernandezia plant," is a type of flowering plant native to the tropical regions of South America, characterized by its unique, pinnately compound leaves. The plant has been found to possess various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in reducing inflammation and preventing infections. Bioactive constituents of Fernandezia falcifolia include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, and to support immune function.
